media.base_medium: M63 minimal medium (quarter strength), full recipe stated inline; no external reference cited

sources_chased.how: none needed — paper defines the complete recipe inline in the Materials and Methods (per-liter composition given in brackets)
checked full text: no trace-element solution or vitamin mix is added; thiamine/vitamin mentions refer to biosynthesis genes, not medium ingredients

media.notes: M63 is a chemically defined minimal medium whose only trace metal is FeSO4 and which contains no vitamin mixture (Ralstonia synthesizes its own cofactors). The paper states the entire per-liter recipe inline, so the medium is complete: glucose (C), (NH4)2SO4 (N + S), KH2PO4 (P/K/buffer), MgSO4 (Mg), FeSO4·7H2O (Fe), pH 7, grown aerobically at 28°C. The target's current_components already covered every real ingredient; oxygen corrected from 'facultative' to 'aerobic' (cultures grown shaking/aerobically).
